name: Sourdough Potato-Rye Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup sourdough starter at room temperature
1 cup potato water at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
DAY ONE:
1. In a large warmed bowl, combine starter, molasses, potato water, and whole-wheat flour.
2. Stir well, cover with plastic wrap and a heavy towel, and let rise in a warm place overnight or for at least 12 hours.
DAY TWO:
1. Bring broth to boil, then add cornmeal and stir constantly until cooked for about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in riced potatoes, cooling them to lukewarm.
2. Add the yeast dissolved in lukewarm water to the starter mixture, along with salt, melted butter, ground mace, and nutmeg.
3. Mix in slowly the rye meal, rye flour, and white flour. The dough will be sticky and heavy.
4. Turn out onto a floured board, adding more flour as needed to prevent sticking, and knead for 8 minutes. If the dough is still sticky after 5 minutes, lightly oil the board and hands, then finish kneading.
5. Form into a smooth ball, place in an oiled bowl coated with towel, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
6. Punch down and turn out onto a floured board; knead for 20 turns. Cover with towel and let rest for 5-10 minutes.
7. Divide into 2 equal portions and form each into a long loaf. Place side by side on a large greased baking sheet.
8. Make 3 slashes from end to end, about 1/2 inch deep.
9. Brush evenly with egg white wash, being careful not to spill onto the baking sheet, and sprinkle generously with poppy seeds.
10. Let rise uncovered in a warm place for 45 minutes or until almost doubled in size.
11. Bake in an oven preheated to 400°F (200°C) for 20 minutes, then reduce heat to 375°F (190°C) and bake for another 30 minutes or until the bread tests done.
12. To give a shiny dark look, place under broiler briefly, being careful not to scorch. Transfer to a wire rack and cool.
